steps.title: Compare expected and found scope
steps.content: Researchers had thought defensive ultrasound was limited to tiger moths, hawkmoths, and one geometer species. Yamamoto and colleagues found ultrasonic clicks in 52 genera, including several geometer genera.

steps.title: Reject extra speculation
steps.content: Choice B introduces other defense strategies, Choice C speculates about moths without bat defenses, and Choice D assigns ultrasonic signaling other purposes. The study instead expands how common anti-bat ultrasound appears to be among large-bodied moths. The answer is A.
